dimensions.rationale: Revision 15 changes only repository checkout and CI stability semantics for text line endings; product behavior, adapter support and release maturity are unchanged.
.gitattributes is assigned to WS-01 as foundation repository configuration. Existing modified paths remain inside WS-08 and WS-14 ownership.
The required post-refreeze gates are current-tree unit tests, neutrality scan, release assembly/verification and ownership audit before push.
The fix preserves strict byte-level content-addressing for synthetic fixtures by preventing checkout conversion instead of weakening fixture digest checks.

findings.message: Windows checkout converted LF text fixtures to CRLF, changing byte size and SHA-256 for content-addressed synthetic fixtures.
findings.requiredAction: Add repository line-ending policy for tracked text files and rerun full local gates before push.

findings.message: The root .gitattributes file required for CI stability was not assigned in the frozen write-set contract.
findings.requiredAction: Assign .gitattributes to WS-01 and refresh the plan lock.

summary: Revision 15 is READY_TO_FREEZE as a minimal Windows CI portability and ownership correction. It changes no product scope, task DAG, adapter maturity, production-promotion boundary or runtime behavior.
